steps.title: Identify the cause-and-effect relationship
steps.content: NASA crashed a probe into Dimorphos, and the next sentence describes what happened as a result: the orbital period changed. The blank needs a word that signals this consequence or result.

steps.title: Match the transition to the relationship
steps.content:
The logical relationship is cause-effect — NASA crashed a probe into Dimorphos, and that impact caused the orbital period to change. The blank needs a transition signaling a result.
  • Choice A — "In addition,": signals a new, separate fact, so fails — the orbital period change is not an independent detail but the direct result of the crash. ✗
  • Choice B — "Specifically,": signals a narrower detail within the same point, so fails — the orbital change is the consequence, not a specification. ✗
  • Choice C — "Consequently,": signals a direct result of what preceded it, so fits. ✓
  • Choice D — "In comparison,": signals a contrast between two things being measured against each other, so fails — no comparison is being drawn. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
